REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, PLAINTIFFS PHILLIP COLLINS, GENESIO LOBATO, THOMAS MCVITTY AND GARY CHEATLE'S AMENDED MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS DENIED. PLAINTIFFS' MOTION FILED ON APRIL 1, 2015 FAILS TO COMPLY WITH CRC 3.1350(B) AND FAILS TO PROVIDE ADEQUATE NOTICE OF THE ISSUE(S) TO BE ADJUDICATED. PLAINTIFFS' ATTEMPT TO CURE THE NON-COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1350(B) BY FILING AND ELECTRONICALLY SERVING AN AMENDED NOTICE 6 WEEKS LATER ON MAY 15, 2015, RAN AFOUL OF CCP 437C(A). ADDITIONALLY, PLAINTIFF FAILED TO SHIFT ITS BURDEN AS NO EVIDENCE IS PROVIDED AND NOT FACTS ARE CITED IN THE SEPARATE STATEMENT REGARDING THE REQUIRED ELEMENT OF THE CAUSE OF ACTION FOR VIOLATION OF CC 1942.4 RE: "DELAY [OF ABATEMENT] IS WITHOUT GOOD CAUSE." CC 1942.4(A)(3). PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312(B). JUDGE RONALD E. QUIDACHAY, CLERK: M. OLOPERNES, NOT REPORTED

MINI MINUTES: No appearances, tentative ruling adopted as follows: PLAINTIFFs PHILLIP COLLINS, GENESIO LOBATO, THOMAS MCVITTY and GARY CHEATLE's AMENDED MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION is DENIED. Plaintiffs' Motion filed on April 1, 2015 fails to comply with CRC 3.1350(b) and fails to provide adequate notice of the issue(s) to be adjudicated. Plaintiffs' attempt to cure the non-compliance with CRC 3.1350(b) by filing and electronically serving an Amended Notice 6 weeks later on May 15, 2015, ran afoul of CCP 437c(a). Additionally, Plaintiff failed to shift its burden as no evidence is provided and not facts are cited in the Separate Statement regarding the required element of the cause of action for violation of CC 1942.4 re: "delay [of abatement] is without good cause." CC 1942.4(a)(3). Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge Ronald E. Quidachay, Clerk: M. Olopernes, Not Reported, D501.

MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
Michael Bracamontes, Esq. of Bracamontes & Vlasak, Tel. 415.835.6777, for plaintiff and moving party;
Ada Y. Lau, Esq. of Clapp Moroney Vucinich Beeman Scheley, Tel. 650.989.5400, for defendant and opposing party.

RULING: Both parties stipulated to the Judge Pro Tem. The matter was argued. At the conclusion of argument, the Court adopted and modified its tentative ruling on PLAINTIFFS PHILLIP COLLINS, GENESIO LOBATO, THOMAS MCVITTY AND GARY CHEATLE'S Motion To Quash Defendants Deposition Subpoenas For Production Of Business Records And Or For Protective Order; Requests For Sanctions, as follows: Plaintiffs' Motion to Quash Defendants' Deposition Subpoenas directed to various charitable and governmental agencies is granted as to all outstanding subpoenas with the exception of those issued on December 18, 2014 as exemplified by Exhibit "A" to the declaration of Paul Sheng. As to those subpoenas, the Motion is granted as to paragraph no. 1 of the request for production of documents as being too broadly worded, and as to paragraph no. 4 of the request for production of documents as it calls for private information not relevant to the issues in the litigation. Under paragraph #3 no medical record will be produced other than those relating to 615 Minna Street, San Francisco, CA. The Court will assess sanctions in the amount of $1,000.00 to be paid to Plaintiffs' counsel within ten (10) days from the date of this order. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge Pro Tem: Paul Renne; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Not reported.
